claim.observation: A recovered registry-import file (winclient.reg, NTFS inode 92256, recycled) contains: [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netman\domain] with values "home"="http://199.73.28.114/ads/" and "pause"=dword:00000020.
claim.interpretation: This .reg file is structured to configure malware command-and-control by writing under the Netman service key (CurrentControlSet\Services\Netman\domain): 'home' holds the C2 callback URL http://199.73.28.114/ads/ and 'pause' (0x20 = 32) appears to hold a beacon/sleep interval. Storing such settings under a legitimate service's subkey (Netman) is consistent with a concealment/persistence technique. This is consistent with http://199.73.28.114/ads/ being the C2 endpoint specified for the implant. As a standalone .reg artifact it documents an intended configuration only; it does not by itself establish that the key was applied to the live registry, nor that the host communicated with that address - corroboration from the implant binary and registry/network artifacts would be needed.
